checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences that are not present in the source PDF. The metadata title 'Map Imagery Photos Tables Screenshots Diagrams Data Charts Ff40' appears to be AI-generated, which is an acceptable change per the instructions.

The output JSON contains the URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21' which is correctly present on page 2 of the source PDF. No other URLs, phone numbers, or addresses are found in the output. The missing URL on page 13 is categorized as an omission (C5) rather than a fabrication.

All 13 pages of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON by at least a heading or an image element. No entire pages or primary sections were skipped entirely, although significant text content within those sections was omitted.
The source document contains no tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis:
The output JSON suffers from multiple significant text omissions where informative labels and descriptions within icons or lists were not extracted as text elements. Specifically: 
1. Page 2: The descriptive text for all icons (e.g., 'Meeting Schedule, Agendas, Minutes and Presentations' for Council Meetings) and the date 'Aug 17, 2022' are missing.
2. Page 3: The list in Element 26 is missing all 6 sub-bullet points under 'Accomplishments' ('Data Governance', 'Public Access to GIS Data', etc.), which represent over 50% of the list content.
3. Page 8: The labels 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE' and 'WORKING GROUP FOR SEAMLESS PARCELS' are missing.
4. Page 9: The labels 'NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION' and 'TAC INFRASTRUCTURE WORKING GROUP' are missing.
5. Page 11: The labels '2020 CENSUS UPDATES AND ASSISTANCE', 'NOAA LAND COVER PILOT', 'ELEVATION DATA', and 'HYDROGRAPHY WORKING GROUP' are missing.
6. Page 12: The labels 'ESRI SPECIAL ACHIEVEMENT AWARD', 'ESRI ENTERPRISE LICENSE AGREEMENT', and 'PROFESSIONAL MEETINGS' are missing.
7. Page 13: The feedback URL 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback' is entirely missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: • Data Governance • Public Access to GIS Data • Collaboration for Land Information • Intergovernmental Collaboration • Sharing Information and Knowledge • Future Priorities (Page 3); ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE, WORKING GROUP FOR SEAMLESS PARCELS (Page 8); NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION, TAC INFRASTRUCTURE WORKING GROUP (Page 9); 2020 CENSUS UPDATES AND ASSISTANCE, NOAA LAND COVER PILOT, ELEVATION DATA, HYDROGRAPHY WORKING GROUP (Page 11); ESRI SPECIAL ACHIEVEMENT AWARD, ESRI ENTERPRISE LICENSE AGREEMENT, PROFESSIONAL MEETINGS (Page 12); www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback (Page 13)
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content for these items is absent from the corresponding sections in the JSON output, often replaced by generic alt-text for images that do not capture the information.
checks.b_evidence_location: Elements 26, 71-72, 78-79, 92-95, 102-104, and the end of the element list for page 13.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Besides the major omissions noted in C5, the text that was successfully extracted (e.g., on Page 1 and page titles) faithfully matches the source content. Minor formatting variations like line merging are acceptable.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for duplicated text blocks across the sections corresponding to the 13 pages of the source PDF. No instances of repeated paragraphs, sections, or list items were found that were not present in the original document.

The heading hierarchy is generally well-maintained. The main title of each page/slide is assigned an H1 level (e.g., 'Outline', 'Highlights: COVID Response'). Section 0 uses an H1 for the report year and H2 for the organization name, which is an acceptable adjustment for accessibility. No heading levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3).
The source document does not contain any tables; the content is primarily organized as text blocks, lists, and icons.
The output follows the logical sequence of pages from 1 to 13 as presented in the source PDF. Within the pages, the arrangement of headings and content follows the visual flow of the slides.

The list on Page 3 ('Outline') is incorrectly converted. The source PDF shows a list with six top-level bullet points, one of which ('Accomplishments') has five nested sub-bullets. The output JSON list contains only the first five top-level items and completely omits the nested sub-bullets and the final top-level item ('Future Priorities'). This significant omission of structural components fails to preserve the meaning and content of the list.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Executive Summary
• About the Council
• FY 20-21 Highlights
• Goals
• Accomplishments
 • Data Governance
 • Public Access to GIS Data
 • Collaboration for Land Information
 • Intergovernmental Collaboration
 • Sharing Information and Knowledge
• Future Priorities

checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture two prominent URLs present in the source PDF. These are not rendered as link or text elements in the semantic layer, though they are visible in the original document. Missing essential functional content like government URLs is a defect.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text elements for these URLs are entirely absent from the corresponding elements arrays in Section 1 and Section 12.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully converts the slide-based presentation into a single-column accessible web format. The visual organization follows the logical flow of the original document. Information from the multi-column sections (like the cover page sidebar and the 'What We're Doing' grid) is correctly serialized and placed in an intuitive order. The transition from a slide layout to a scrolling page layout is handled without loss of structural meaning.

There are no traditional data tables in the source document. The layout elements that resemble grids (like 'What We're Doing' on page 2) are rendered as individual content blocks in the HTML, which is an appropriate conversion for this content type. All text within these blocks remains readable and associated with its respective icon or image.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the large aerial photograph on the cover, the icons and photographs in the 'What We're Doing' section, the 'nc ONE map' logo, the various circular icons used as bullet points in the 'Collaboration' and 'Sharing' sections, and the 'Questions?' image on the final page. The agency logo (NCDIT) is also consistently present where expected.

There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the body of the document. Each section's unique content (text and images) appears once in the appropriate sequence. While the agency logo (NCDIT) appears multiple times, this is a faithful representation of its appearance at the bottom of every slide in the source PDF. The table of contents at the top contains navigation links, which is a standard accessibility feature and not a duplication of content.
